instant.evidence: Interactive chatbot: answer validation is a small bounded LLM call (max ~300 tokens) and the simulation timeline streams events; responses are prompt.

natural.evidence: Conversational 10-question intake with LLM answer validation (complete/partial/off-topic) and natural-language blueprint output.

permitted.evidence: Public-by-design lead funnel: anonymous intake, but the deliverable (PDF/results) is gated behind lead capture, the enterprise webhook is HMAC-signed, and calendar writes use a scoped service account. See RESIDUAL note.

adaptive.evidence: Taxonomy registry caches and LLM-syncs new industries on cache miss; maturity scoring adapts recommended outcomes to the answers given.

contextual.evidence: Grounds recommendations in the persisted Q&A corpus + industry taxonomy + the 40+ capability catalog rather than free-form generation.

transparent.evidence: Append-only event log (output/advisory/_events_log.json) + per-session state snapshots (advisory_state.json) + surfaced system-confidence and maturity scores.

governance.evidence: Session state machine prevents re-generation; deliverable gated behind lead capture; per-subsystem degradation. See RESIDUAL note re: kill-switch.

observability.evidence: Event log + session-state snapshots + INFO logs for each lifecycle stage (started/questions_completed/results_generated/lead_captured).

availability.evidence: Graceful degradation: LLM unavailable -> safe keyword-only defaults; calendar key absent -> static slots; webhook URL absent -> no-op. Atomic temp+rename writes.

lexicon.evidence: Consistent advisory vocabulary (Revenue/Operations/Customer Engine systems, capability catalog ids, 5 maturity dimensions). Enforced fleet-wide by the canonical glossary (config/lexicon.json) via the deterministic checker execution/ops_platform/lexicon.py and the scripts/lexicon_check.py CI gate; this artifact passes with zero forbidden-term or drift violations.

solid.evidence: LLM calls wrapped in try/except with fallbacks; lead upsert-by-email prevents duplicates; webhook is fire-and-forget non-blocking; atomic writes.

layers.name: Unified Semantic Layer
layers.how: Industry taxonomy + capability catalog give shared meaning to free-text answers.

layers.name: Intelligent Retrieval (RAG)
layers.how: Grounds the blueprint in the Q&A corpus + taxonomy + capability catalog.

layers.name: Agent-Aware Governance
layers.how: Lead-gated deliverable; HMAC-signed downstream webhook; scoped calendar service account.

layers.name: Observability & Feedback
layers.how: Event log + per-session state snapshots + confidence/maturity surfaced.

notes: RESIDUAL RISK (HIGH), tracked in docs/audits/tbi-fleet-audit.md: this is a PUBLIC unauthenticated endpoint that creates Basecamp projects and fires enterprise webhooks, with no single kill-switch (only per-subsystem env degradation). Compliant on the framework dimensions (the access model is an intentional lead-funnel design with gated deliverable + signed/scoped downstream), but the hardening follow-up is: add an OPS_ADVISORY_ENABLED kill-switch + an access review. Representative entrypoint; the same posture covers answer_validator.py, taxonomy_registry.py, and the generation engines.
